```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

# Input and Output Directories
metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir    <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create the Plots directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD REGIONAL MASTER DATA
# =====================================================================
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 3. DEFINE REGIONAL Q-Q PLOTTING FUNCTION (Calib = Black, Valid by Scale)
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_qq <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter predictions for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual)) %>% 
    mutate(
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calibration data", "Validation data"),
      # Convert Scale_k to a factor and sort it so the legend is in numeric order
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot data to long format so we can facet by Model
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Scale_k_factor", "Statistic", "Actual", "Data_Type", "Station_ID"))
  
  df_long <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted))
  
  if(nrow(df_long) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 3. Find global min/max so the X and Y axes perfectly match (true 1:1 plot)
  min_val <- min(c(df_long$Actual, df_long$Predicted), na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(c(df_long$Actual, df_long$Predicted), na.rm = TRUE)
  buffer <- (max_val - min_val) * 0.05
  if(buffer == 0) buffer <- 0.05
  
  # 4. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, shape = Data_Type)) +
    # The 1:1 perfect agreement line
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.8) +
    
    # Layer 1: Calibration Points (Strictly Black)
    geom_point(data = subset(df_long, Data_Type == "Calibration data"), 
               color = "black", alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
    
    # Layer 2: Validation Points (Colored by Scale k)
    geom_point(data = subset(df_long, Data_Type == "Validation data"), 
               aes(color = Scale_k_factor), alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
    
    # Create separate panels for each model 
    facet_wrap(~ Model) + 
    
    # Formatting
    scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Valid. Scale (k)", option = "turbo") + 
    scale_shape_manual(name = "Data Type", values = c("Calibration data" = 16, "Validation data" = 17)) +
    
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(min_val - buffer, max_val + buffer)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(min_val - buffer, max_val + buffer)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional Empirical vs Predicted:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = bquote("Empirical (Actual)" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      y = bquote("Predicted" ~ .(y_label[[1]]))
    )  +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dotted", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right", 
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    # Ensure point colors override alpha transparency in the legend
    guides(
      color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 3), order = 1),
      # Set legend colors to black for Calib and gray for Valid so the shapes represent the map accurately
      shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = c("black", "gray50"), alpha = 1, size = 3), order = 2)
    )
  
  return(p)
}
# =====================================================================
# 4. GENERATE AND EXPORT THE PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Q-Q plots...\n")

qq_p_zero <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
qq_t2     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
qq_t3     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
qq_t4     <- plot_regional_qq(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
qq_list <- list(qq_p_zero, qq_t2, qq_t3, qq_t4)
qq_list <- qq_list[!sapply(qq_list, is.null)] # Remove empty plots safely

if(length(qq_list) > 0) {
  combined_qq_plots <- wrap_plots(qq_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_QQ_Validation_Plots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_qq_plots, width = 14, height = 12, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales) # Needed to extract the turbo palette colors

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES (Assuming master data is already loaded)
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
# all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE MSE BARPLOT FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_mse_bars <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Statistic", "Actual", "Station_ID", "Data_Type"))
  
  # 3. Calculate Regional MSE per Scale and Model
  df_mse <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    group_by(Scale_k, Model) %>%
    summarize(MSE = mean((Actual - Predicted)^2, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calib. data", "Valid. data"),
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_mse) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Create Custom Color Palette
  # Validation (< 24) gets vibrant colors; Calibration (>= 24) gets grey
  unique_scales <- sort(unique(df_mse$Scale_k))
  valid_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales < 24]
  calib_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales >= 24]
  
  # Extract hex codes from the "turbo" palette for validation scales
  valid_colors <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "turbo")(length(valid_scales))
  calib_colors <- rep("grey60", length(calib_scales)) # uniform grey for training
  
  # Map scales to colors
  custom_colors <- setNames(c(valid_colors, calib_colors), 
                            c(as.character(valid_scales), as.character(calib_scales)))
  
  # 5. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_mse, aes(x = Model, y = MSE, fill = Scale_k_factor)) +
    # Group bars by Model, dodge by Scale_k
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.85), width = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
    
    # Apply custom fill colors
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Scale k (hours)", values = custom_colors) +
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional MSE by Model:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = "Theoretical Model",
      y = "Mean Squared Error (MSE)"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), # Remove vertical grid lines for cleaner bars
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, vjust = 1, hjust = 1) # Slight tilt for model names
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional MSE Barplots...\n")

bar_p_zero <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
bar_t2     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
bar_t3     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
bar_t4     <- plot_regional_mse_bars(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
bar_list <- list(bar_p_zero, bar_t2, bar_t3, bar_t4)
bar_list <- bar_list[!sapply(bar_list, is.null)] # Remove empty plots safely

if(length(bar_list) > 0) {
  combined_bar_plots <- wrap_plots(bar_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "right") # Collect the legend on the right
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_MSE_Barplots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_bar_plots, width = 14, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined MSE bar plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales) 

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE SQUARED ERROR BOXPLOT FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_regional_se_boxplots <- function(df, stat_name, y_label) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- setdiff(names(df_stat), c("Scale_k", "Statistic", "Actual", "Station_ID", "Data_Type"))
  
  # 3. Calculate Squared Error per Station, Scale, and Model
  # We do NOT summarize here, so we keep the distribution for the boxplot
  df_se <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    mutate(
      Squared_Error = (Actual - Predicted)^2,
      Data_Type = ifelse(Scale_k >= 24, "Calib. data", "Valid. data"),
      Scale_k_factor = factor(Scale_k, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k)))
    )
  
  if(nrow(df_se) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Create Custom Color Palette
  # Validation (< 24) gets vibrant colors; Calibration (>= 24) gets grey
  unique_scales <- sort(unique(df_se$Scale_k))
  valid_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales < 24]
  calib_scales <- unique_scales[unique_scales >= 24]
  
  valid_colors <- scales::viridis_pal(option = "turbo")(length(valid_scales))
  calib_colors <- rep("grey60", length(calib_scales)) 
  
  # Map scales to colors
  custom_colors <- setNames(c(valid_colors, calib_colors), 
                            c(as.character(valid_scales), as.character(calib_scales)))
  
  # 5. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_se, aes(x = Model, y = Squared_Error, fill = Scale_k_factor)) +
    # Group boxplots by Model, dodge by Scale_k
    geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.85), width = 0.7, 
                 outlier.size = 0.5, outlier.alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.3) +
    
    # Apply custom fill colors
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Scale k (hours)", values = custom_colors) +
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Regional SE Dist.:" ~ .(y_label[[1]])),
      x = "Theoretical Model",
      y = "Squared Error (SE)"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), 
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
      legend.position = "right",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, vjust = 1, hjust = 1) 
    )
  
  # NOTE: Squared Errors often have massive outliers. 
  # If your boxes are getting squashed flat, uncomment the next line to use a logarithmic scale:
  # p <- p + scale_y_log10() + ylab("Squared Error (Log10 Scale)")
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional SE Boxplots...\n")

box_p_zero <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]^{(k)}))
box_t2     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(t[2]^{(k)}))
box_t3     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(t[3]^{(k)}))
box_t4     <- plot_regional_se_boxplots(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(t[4]^{(k)}))

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork
box_list <- list(box_p_zero, box_t2, box_t3, box_t4)
box_list <- box_list[!sapply(box_list, is.null)] 

if(length(box_list) > 0) {
  combined_box_plots <- wrap_plots(box_list, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
    theme(legend.position = "right") 
  
  # Save the large validation grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_SE_Boxplots.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_box_plots, width = 14, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined SE boxplots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the master data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)

# Extract the evaluated models dataframe (contains mse_validation)
eval_models <- master_regional_data$Final_Evaluated_Models

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE ECDF PLOTTING FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_ecdf_mse <- function(df, stat_name, title_expr) {
  
  # 1. Filter for the specific statistic and remove NA validation MSEs
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(mse_validation))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Ensure models are factors so the legend order and mapping are consistent
  expected_models <- c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p")
  df_stat$Model <- factor(df_stat$Model, levels = expected_models)
  
  
  
  # 4. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_stat, aes(x = mse_validation, color = Model, linetype = Model)) +
    # Use step function for ECDF
    stat_ecdf(geom = "step", linewidth = 1) +
    
    # Map colors to match the uploaded image (Wei = Blue, Lom/PowerLaw = Red)
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "blue", "Weibull_2p" = "blue",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "red", "PowerLaw_2p" = "red"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    # Map linetypes to match the uploaded image (1p = Solid, 2p = Dashed)
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "solid", "Weibull_2p" = "dashed",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "solid", "PowerLaw_2p" = "dashed"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    
    labs(
      title = bquote("Statistic:" ~ .(title_expr[[1]])),
      x = expression(epsilon ~ "(MSE)"),
      y = expression("Prob(" * epsilon <= x * ")")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      
      # Boxed legend styling similar to the image
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.8, 0.2), # Adjust coordinates if legend overlaps curves
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional ECDF Plots...\n")

ecdf_p_zero <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "p_zero", expression(p[0]))
ecdf_t2     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t2_pos", expression(tau[2]^"+"))
ecdf_t3     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t3_pos", expression(tau[3]^"+"))
ecdf_t4     <- plot_ecdf_mse(eval_models, "t4_pos", expression(tau[4]^"+"))

# Remove the legend from all plots except the first one for a cleaner grid
if (!is.null(ecdf_t2)) ecdf_t2 <- ecdf_t2 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_t3)) ecdf_t3 <- ecdf_t3 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_t4)) ecdf_t4 <- ecdf_t4 + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork (1 row, 4 columns)
ecdf_list <- list(ecdf_p_zero, ecdf_t2, ecdf_t3, ecdf_t4)
ecdf_list <- ecdf_list[!sapply(ecdf_list, is.null)] 

if(length(ecdf_list) > 0) {
  combined_ecdf_plots <- wrap_plots(ecdf_list, nrow = 1) 
  
  # Save the grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_ECDF_MSE.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_ecdf_plots, width = 16, height = 4.5, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined ECDF plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your Final_Evaluated_Models data.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# (If you need to reload the master data, uncomment the lines below)
# metadata_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
# master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
# master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)

# Extract all predictions to calculate errors per scale dynamically
all_preds <- master_regional_data$All_Predictions

# =====================================================================
# 2. DEFINE APE ECDF PLOTTING FUNCTION
# =====================================================================
plot_ecdf_ape <- function(df, stat_name, title_expr) {
  
  # 1. Filter for specific statistic and VALIDATION scales only (< 24)
  df_stat <- df %>% 
    filter(Statistic == stat_name, Scale_k < 24) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Actual))
  
  if(nrow(df_stat) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 2. Pivot to long format to access all Models
  model_cols <- intersect(names(df_stat), c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p"))
  
  df_long <- df_stat %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_cols), names_to = "Model", values_to = "Predicted") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Predicted)) %>%
    
    # 3. Calculate Absolute Percentage Error (APE) based on formula
    mutate(APE = 100 * abs(Predicted - Actual) / Actual) %>%
    
    # Remove Inf/NaNs just in case an Actual value was exactly 0
    filter(is.finite(APE))
  
  if(nrow(df_long) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Ensure models are factors so the legend order is consistent
  expected_models <- c("Weibull_1p", "Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_1p", "PowerLaw_2p")
  df_long$Model <- factor(df_long$Model, levels = expected_models)
  

  
  # 6. Generate the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = APE, color = Model, linetype = Model)) +
    # Step function for ECDF
    stat_ecdf(geom = "step", linewidth = 1) +
    
    # Vertical reference line at x = 5 (matching your image)
    geom_vline(xintercept = 5, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    
    # Map colors to match the uploaded image (Wei = Blue, Lom/PowerLaw = Red)
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "blue", "Weibull_2p" = "blue",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "red", "PowerLaw_2p" = "red"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    
    # Map linetypes to match the uploaded image (1p = Solid, 2p = Dashed)
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("Weibull_1p" = "solid", "Weibull_2p" = "dashed",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "solid", "PowerLaw_2p" = "dashed"),
      labels = c("Weibull_1p" = "Wei-1p", "Weibull_2p" = "Wei-2p",
                 "PowerLaw_1p" = "Lom-1p", "PowerLaw_2p" = "Lom-2p")
    ) +
    

    labs(
      title = bquote("Statistic:" ~ .(title_expr[[1]])),
      x = expression(epsilon ~ "[%]"),
      y = expression("Prob(" * epsilon <= x * ")")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "lightgray"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      
      # Boxed legend styling
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.75, 0.25), 
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE AND EXPORT PLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Regional APE ECDF Plots...\n")

ecdf_ape_p_zero <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "p_zero", expression(p[0]))
ecdf_ape_t2     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t2_pos", expression(tau[2]^"+"))
ecdf_ape_t3     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t3_pos", expression(tau[3]^"+"))
ecdf_ape_t4     <- plot_ecdf_ape(all_preds, "t4_pos", expression(tau[4]^"+"))

# Remove the legend from all plots except the first one for a cleaner grid
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t2)) ecdf_ape_t2 <- ecdf_ape_t2 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t3)) ecdf_ape_t3 <- ecdf_ape_t3 + theme(legend.position = "none")
if (!is.null(ecdf_ape_t4)) ecdf_ape_t4 <- ecdf_ape_t4 + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine them into a grid using patchwork (1 row, 4 columns)
ecdf_ape_list <- list(ecdf_ape_p_zero, ecdf_ape_t2, ecdf_ape_t3, ecdf_ape_t4)
ecdf_ape_list <- ecdf_ape_list[!sapply(ecdf_ape_list, is.null)] 

if(length(ecdf_ape_list) > 0) {
  combined_ecdf_ape_plots <- wrap_plots(ecdf_ape_list, nrow = 1) 
  
  # Save the grid
  output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_ECDF_APE.png")
  ggsave(output_file, combined_ecdf_ape_plots, width = 16, height = 4.5, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  
  cat("Success! Combined APE ECDF plots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: No plots were generated. Check your All_Predictions data.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(sf)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

metadata_dir  <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")
maps_dir      <- file.path(gdrive_path, "General_Data/Maps", data_dir_name)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA
# =====================================================================
cat("Loading Data...\n")

# Load Master Regional Data
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
final_summary <- master_regional_data$Final_Summary

# Load Metadata to get Station Coordinates
meta_xlsx_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_Metadata.xlsx"))
metadata_coords <- read_xlsx(meta_xlsx_path) %>%
  select(Station_ID, Latitude, Longitude)

# Define the statistics we want to analyze
stats_of_interest <- c("p_zero", "l1_all", "l1_pos", "t2_pos", "t3_pos", "t4_pos")

# Filter final_summary for just the needed columns
df_stats <- final_summary %>%
  select(Station_ID, k_hours, all_of(stats_of_interest))

# =====================================================================
# 3. TASK 1: EXPORT EXCEL & GENERATE BOXPLOTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Exporting Data to Excel...\n")
# Export the clean summary values to Excel
excel_out_path <- file.path(plots_dir, "Empirical_Statistics_All_Scales.xlsx")
write_xlsx(df_stats, excel_out_path)
cat("Excel saved to:", excel_out_path, "\n")

cat("Generating Empirical Boxplots...\n")
# Pivot data to long format for faceted boxplots
df_long <- df_stats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(stats_of_interest), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Scale_k_factor = factor(k_hours, levels = sort(unique(k_hours)))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Value))

# Generate the massive boxplot grid
p_box <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Scale_k_factor, y = Value, fill = Scale_k_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5, outlier.alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ Statistic, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Empirical Statistics Distribution Across Temporal Scales",
    x = "Scale k (hours)",
    y = "Statistic Value"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Save the Boxplots
ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "Empirical_Statistics_Boxplots.png"), p_box, width = 12, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)

# =====================================================================
# 4. TASK 2: GENERATE MAPS FOR ALL SCALES
# =====================================================================
cat("Preparing Spatial Data for Maps...\n")

# Find the shapefile in your maps directory
shp_file <- list.files(maps_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE)[1]
if (is.na(shp_file)) {
  stop("Could not find a .shp file in the specified maps_dir!")
}

# Read the shapefile and force CRS to EPSG:3857
germany_shp <- st_read(shp_file, quiet = TRUE) %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 3857)

# Join the statistics with the coordinates
map_data <- df_stats %>%
  left_join(metadata_coords, by = "Station_ID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Latitude) & !is.na(Longitude))

# Convert the dataframe to an sf object
# We assume standard lat/lon are WGS84 (EPSG:4326), then transform to match shapefile (EPSG:3857)
map_sf <- st_as_sf(map_data, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3857)

# Loop through each statistic and create a map faceted by temporal scale
for (stat in stats_of_interest) {
  cat("Generating maps for:", stat, "...\n")
  
  # Filter out NAs for the specific statistic to avoid blank gray dots
  plot_sf <- map_sf %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[stat]]))
  
  p_map <- ggplot() +
    # Layer 1: The Germany Base Map
    geom_sf(data = germany_shp, fill = "whitesmoke", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.3) +
    
    # Layer 2: The Station Points mapped by the statistic value
    geom_sf(data = plot_sf, aes(color = .data[[stat]]), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Facet by scale
    facet_wrap(~ k_hours, ncol = 5) +
    
    # Aesthetics
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "turbo", name = stat) +
    theme_void() + # Removes axes, lat/lon grids, and background
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, margin = margin(b = 5, t = 5)),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18, margin = margin(b = 15)),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Spatial Distribution of", stat, "Across Scales"),
      subtitle = "Coordinate Reference System: EPSG:3857"
    )
  
  # Save the mapped facets
  map_out_file <- file.path(plots_dir, paste0("Spatial_Map_", stat, ".png"))
  ggsave(map_out_file, p_map, width = 16, height = 12, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
}

cat("\n--- ALL TASKS COMPLETE ---\n")
```



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

metadata_dir  <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Create a subfolder for these specific spatial scatter plots to keep things organized
spatial_scatter_dir <- file.path(plots_dir, "Spatial_Scatters_XYZ")
dir.create(spatial_scatter_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA & EXTRACT X, Y, Z
# =====================================================================
cat("Loading Data...\n")

# Load Master Regional Data
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
final_summary <- master_regional_data$Final_Summary

# Load Metadata to get X (Longitude), Y (Latitude), and Z (Elevation_m)
meta_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_Metadata.rds"))
metadata_xyz <- readRDS(meta_rds_path) %>%
  select(Station_ID, Longitude, Latitude, Elevation_m)

# Merge the summary statistics with the spatial coordinates
df_spatial <- final_summary %>%
  left_join(metadata_xyz, by = "Station_ID")

# Define the statistics and geographic variables
stats_of_interest <- c("p_zero", "l1_all", "l1_pos", "t2_pos", "t3_pos", "t4_pos")
geo_vars <- list(
  "Longitude"   = list(label = "X (Longitude)", color = "#1b9e77"),
  "Latitude"    = list(label = "Y (Latitude)", color = "#d95f02"),
  "Elevation_m" = list(label = "Z (Elevation, meters)", color = "#7570b3")
)

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE SCATTER PLOTS: STATISTIC VS X, Y, Z
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Spatial XYZ Scatter Plots...\n")

# Loop through each statistic
for (stat in stats_of_interest) {
  
  # Filter out missing values for the current statistic
  df_stat <- df_spatial %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[stat]])) %>%
    mutate(Scale_Label = paste0(k_hours, "h")) %>%
    mutate(Scale_Label = factor(Scale_Label, levels = paste0(sort(unique(k_hours)), "h")))
  
  # Loop through X, Y, Z
  for (geo_name in names(geo_vars)) {
    geo_label <- geo_vars[[geo_name]]$label
    geo_color <- geo_vars[[geo_name]]$color
    
    # Generate the scatter plot faceted by scale
    p_scatter <- ggplot(df_stat, aes(x = .data[[geo_name]], y = .data[[stat]])) +
      # Points with transparency to handle overlapping stations
      geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5, color = geo_color) +
      
      # Add a linear trendline to visualize geographic/elevation relationships
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, se = FALSE) +
      
      # Facet by temporal scale
      facet_wrap(~ Scale_Label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
      
      labs(
        title = paste("Relationship:", stat, "vs", geo_label),
        subtitle = "Across all available temporal scales (k)",
        x = geo_label,
        y = stat
      ) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "darkgray"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      )
    
    # Save the plot
    out_name <- paste0("Scatter_", stat, "_vs_", geo_name, ".png")
    ggsave(file.path(spatial_scatter_dir, out_name), p_scatter, width = 14, height = 10, bg = "white", dpi = 300)
  }
}

cat("Success! All XYZ scatter plots saved to:\n", spatial_scatter_dir, "\n")
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(sf)
library(writexl)
library(readxl)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

metadata_dir  <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")
maps_dir      <- file.path(gdrive_path, "General_Data/Maps", data_dir_name)

# Ensure subdirectories exist
xyz_dir <- file.path(plots_dir, "Spatial_Scatters_XYZ")
dir.create(xyz_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD MASTER DATA & METADATA
# =====================================================================
cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
final_summary <- master_regional_data$Final_Summary

# Load Metadata to get Station Coordinates and Elevation
meta_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_Metadata.rds"))
metadata_full <- readRDS(meta_rds_path) %>%
  select(Station_ID, Longitude, Latitude, Elevation_m)

# Define the statistics to analyze
stats_of_interest <- c("p_zero", "l1_all", "l1_pos", "t2_pos", "t3_pos", "t4_pos")

# Merge empirical statistics with spatial coordinates
df_combined <- final_summary %>%
  select(Station_ID, k_hours, all_of(stats_of_interest)) %>%
  left_join(metadata_full, by = "Station_ID")

# =====================================================================
# 3. TASK 1: BOXPLOT STATISTICS EXPORT
# =====================================================================
cat("Calculating and Exporting Boxplot Statistics...\n")

df_long_stats <- df_combined %>%
  select(Station_ID, k_hours, all_of(stats_of_interest)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(stats_of_interest), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Value))

boxplot_stats_df <- df_long_stats %>%
  group_by(Statistic, k_hours) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = n(),
    Min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1_25th = quantile(Value, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3_75th = quantile(Value, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3_75th - Q1_25th,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Statistic, k_hours)

write_xlsx(boxplot_stats_df, file.path(plots_dir, "Empirical_Boxplot_Statistics.xlsx"))

# =====================================================================
# 4. TASK 2: GENERATE MAPS FOR ALL SCALES
# =====================================================================
cat("Preparing Spatial Data for Maps...\n")

# Load shapefile
shp_file <- list.files(maps_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE)[1]
if (is.na(shp_file)) {
  warning("Shapefile not found! Skipping map generation.")
} else {
  germany_shp <- st_read(shp_file, quiet = TRUE) %>% 
    st_transform(crs = 3857)
  
  # Convert data to sf object (Assuming original coords are EPSG:4326)
  map_sf <- df_combined %>% filter(!is.na(Longitude) & !is.na(Latitude))
  map_sf <- st_as_sf(map_sf, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
    st_transform(crs = 3857)
  
  for (stat in stats_of_interest) {
    cat("  Mapping:", stat, "...\n")
    plot_sf <- map_sf %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[stat]]))
    
    p_map <- ggplot() +
      geom_sf(data = germany_shp, fill = "whitesmoke", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.3) +
      geom_sf(data = plot_sf, aes(color = .data[[stat]]), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~ k_hours, ncol = 5) +
      scale_color_viridis_c(option = "turbo", name = stat) +
      theme_void() + 
      theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 5, t = 5)),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 10))
      ) +
      labs(title = paste("Spatial Distribution:", stat))
    
    ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, paste0("Map_", stat, ".png")), p_map, width = 16, height = 12, bg = "white")
  }
}

# =====================================================================
# 5. TASK 3: XYZ SCATTER PLOTS WITH REGRESSION (Plot + Excel)
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating XYZ Scatters and Calculating Regressions...\n")

geo_vars <- list(
  "Longitude"   = list(label = "X (Longitude)", color = "#1b9e77"),
  "Latitude"    = list(label = "Y (Latitude)", color = "#d95f02"),
  "Elevation_m" = list(label = "Z (Elevation, meters)", color = "#7570b3")
)

# List to store regression results for Excel export
all_regressions <- list()

for (stat in stats_of_interest) {
  df_stat <- df_combined %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[stat]])) %>%
    mutate(Scale_Label = paste0(k_hours, "h")) %>%
    mutate(Scale_Label = factor(Scale_Label, levels = paste0(sort(unique(k_hours)), "h")))
  
  for (geo_name in names(geo_vars)) {
    geo_label <- geo_vars[[geo_name]]$label
    geo_color <- geo_vars[[geo_name]]$color
    
    # 5A: Calculate Linear Models per Scale to overlay text & export to Excel
    lm_results <- df_stat %>%
      group_by(k_hours, Scale_Label) %>%
      summarise(
        n_stations = n(),
        # Fit linear model safely
        fit = list(lm(as.formula(paste(stat, "~", geo_name)), data = cur_data())),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      rowwise() %>%
      mutate(
        Intercept = coef(fit)[1],
        Slope     = coef(fit)[2],
        R_squared = summary(fit)$r.squared,
        # Format the text equation for the plot: y = mx + c
        sign_c    = ifelse(Intercept >= 0, "+", "-"),
        eq_label  = sprintf("y = %.3fx %s %.3f\nR² = %.3f", Slope, sign_c, abs(Intercept), R_squared)
      ) %>%
      select(-fit, -sign_c) %>% # Clean up
      mutate(Statistic = stat, Predictor = geo_name) 
    
    # Save to global list for Excel
    all_regressions[[paste(stat, geo_name, sep="_")]] <- lm_results
    
    # 5B: Generate Plot
    p_scatter <- ggplot(df_stat, aes(x = .data[[geo_name]], y = .data[[stat]])) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5, color = geo_color) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, se = FALSE) +
      
      # Add the Equation and R2 text to the top-right of each facet
      geom_text(data = lm_results, aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = eq_label), 
                hjust = 1.05, vjust = 1.2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
      
      facet_wrap(~ Scale_Label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Relationship:", stat, "vs", geo_label),
        x = geo_label,
        y = stat
      ) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "whitesmoke"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
      )
    
    out_name <- paste0("Scatter_", stat, "_vs_", geo_name, ".png")
    ggsave(file.path(xyz_dir, out_name), p_scatter, width = 16, height = 12, bg = "white")
  }
}

# 5C: Combine all regression summaries and export to Excel
regression_master_df <- bind_rows(all_regressions) %>%
  select(Statistic, Predictor, k_hours, n_stations, Slope, Intercept, R_squared, eq_label) %>%
  arrange(Statistic, Predictor, k_hours)

write_xlsx(regression_master_df, file.path(xyz_dir, "XYZ_Regression_Statistics.xlsx"))

cat("\n--- ALL TASKS COMPLETE ---\n")
cat("- Boxplot Data saved to:", plots_dir, "\n")
cat("- Maps saved to:", plots_dir, "\n")
cat("- Scatters and Regression Data saved to:", xyz_dir, "\n")
```




```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 

metadata_dir  <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")
plots_dir     <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")

# Ensure plots directory exists
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA
# =====================================================================
cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))

# Read the updated master object
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)

# Extract the new Scale Data Loss dataframe
df_data_loss <- master_regional_data$Scale_Data_Loss

# =====================================================================
# 3. GENERATE BOXPLOTS FOR DATA LOSS
# =====================================================================
cat("Generating Data Loss Boxplots...\n")

# Prepare the data by ensuring the scale is treated as a sorted factor
df_plot <- df_data_loss %>%
  mutate(Scale_factor = factor(Scale_k_hours, levels = sort(unique(Scale_k_hours)))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Dropped_Pct))

# Generate the plot
p_loss <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Scale_factor, y = Dropped_Pct, fill = Scale_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5, outlier.alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.4) +
  
  # Keep styling consistent with your previous plots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", guide = "none") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Data Loss During Temporal Aggregation",
    subtitle = "Percentage of theoretical bins dropped due to exceeding NA thresholds",
    x = "Scale k (hours)",
    y = "Dropped Bins (%)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "darkgray"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), # Cleaner look for boxplots
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# =====================================================================
# 4. EXPORT PLOT
# =====================================================================
output_file <- file.path(plots_dir, "Regional_Data_Loss_Boxplots.png")
ggsave(output_file, p_loss, width = 12, height = 7, bg = "white", dpi = 300)

cat("Success! Data loss boxplots saved to:\n", output_file, "\n")
```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)

# =====================================================================
# 1. DEFINE PATHS & DIRECTORIES 
# =====================================================================
gdrive_path   <- "G:/My Drive"
project_path  <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
data_dir_name <- "QC_d data - Germany" 
metadata_dir  <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Metadata")

# =====================================================================
# 2. LOAD DATA
# =====================================================================
cat("Loading Master Regional Data...\n")
master_rds_path <- file.path(metadata_dir, paste0(data_dir_name, "_Master_List_Filtered.rds"))
master_regional_data <- readRDS(master_rds_path)
df_data_loss <- master_regional_data$Scale_Data_Loss

# =====================================================================
# 3. CALCULATE OUTLIERS & IQR COUNTS
# =====================================================================
cat("Calculating Station Distributions...\n")

outlier_summary_df <- df_data_loss %>%
  filter(!is.na(Dropped_Pct)) %>%
  group_by(Scale_k_hours) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Stations = n(),
    
    # Calculate Boxplot Boundaries
    Q1 = quantile(Dropped_Pct, 0.25),
    Q3 = quantile(Dropped_Pct, 0.75),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    Lower_Bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR,
    Upper_Bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR,
    
    # Count Stations in each zone
    Inside_IQR_Box = sum(Dropped_Pct >= Q1 & Dropped_Pct <= Q3),
    Total_Outliers = sum(Dropped_Pct < Lower_Bound | Dropped_Pct > Upper_Bound),
    Outliers_High  = sum(Dropped_Pct > Upper_Bound),
    Outliers_Low   = sum(Dropped_Pct < Lower_Bound),
    
    # Non-outliers are total stations minus the outliers
    Inside_Whiskers_Normal = Total_Stations - Total_Outliers,
    
    # Calculate Outlier Percentage
    Outlier_Percentage = round((Total_Outliers / Total_Stations) * 100, 2),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Scale_k_hours)

# =====================================================================
# 4. EXPORT TO EXCEL
# =====================================================================
plots_dir <- file.path(project_path, "Marginals", data_dir_name, "Plots")
excel_out_path <- file.path(plots_dir, "Data_Loss_Outlier_Summary.xlsx")

write_xlsx(outlier_summary_df, excel_out_path)

cat("Success! Outlier summary table exported to:\n", excel_out_path, "\n")
```
